mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount factors, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0 percent, extreme labor market tightness forces rapid wage growth that boosts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in greater than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE falls below 4.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer prices and deceleration in retail sales indicates demand destruction from inflation, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint CPI rise and retail sales drop fails to predict negative 60-day returns with Rank IC greater than 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.0 percent quarter-over-quarter signals robust supply-side capacity that supports corporate margins, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 1.0 percent correlates with negative 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns with statistical significance at p-value less than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 5.0 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 3.0 percent, delayed monetary tightening triggers equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 50 percent of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total payrolls and rising unemployment rate indicates labor force participation collapse, signaling economic fragility that drives negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll-unemployment divergence shows no predictive power for 20-day returns with Rank IC less than 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days during early-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: GDP and retail sales growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns in early-cycle periods with p-value less than 0.05.
